query: Someone on our research team keeps raving about something called Daydream Dictation. What is it and how would I actually use it for policy briefs?
Ok I just installed the plugin, Wispr Flow is running on my phone, and I'm outside. What do I actually do to start my first session for this grant application?
I tried a dictation session for a zoning memo yesterday and I kept glancing at what you were writing. I lost my flow every time. How do I get past that?
Why does this process have three phases? Can't I just have one long conversation with you like normal?
I just finished about 40 minutes of talking through the campaign messaging document. What am I supposed to do now?
I'm at this github pull request page you keep sending me. I have no idea what I'm looking at. Can you walk me through this phase?
What's a gap analysis? My colleague mentioned I should run one on my grant narrative but I don't know what that does in this workflow.
I keep getting lost in Phase 2. What am I actually trying to accomplish when I'm responding to your questions?
I'm a policy writer and I'm totally new to any kind of AI-assisted workflow. How does this thing actually work, start to finish?
I'm halfway through dictating to you about my zoning memo and I'm not sure if I should stop and read what you wrote or keep going. how do I know when to move on to the next phase?
I'm starting my Phase 2 session now for the transportation grant proposal. Let's talk about the overall funding request and why this project matters.
Add a section on stakeholder engagement to the zoning memo, it should cover the community workshops we held in March.
Can you help me structure the executive summary for this RFP response? I have the content, just need help with the shape.
Run a gap analysis on this campaign messaging document — I want to know what I'm missing before I send it to the comms director.
Let's set up a new Daydream project called 'Parks Budget FY27'. I want to start working through the rationale for the aquatics expansion.
How do I squash the last three commits on this branch? I want to consolidate them before I push.
Can you install the wispr flow plugin for me? I'm having trouble getting it to work with my voice dictation setup.
What's the best voice-to-text app for Mac right now? I'm tired of the built-in dictation being bad.
How do skills work in Claude Code? Can I install a skill from a local directory without publishing it?
I wrote up a draft memo on my own and I want your feedback on the argument structure. Nothing fancy, just read it and tell me what's weak.
Do I really need Wispr Flow or can I get by with my laptop's built-in voice typing for these brainstorming sessions?
